content: Patient presented to the emergency department
Patient's main objection to warfarin was the need for frequent monitoring and dose adjustment
span: patient's main objection to warfarin was the need for frequent monitoring and dose adjustment

content: Rivaroxaban was initiated during this admission
Patient was on triple antithrombotic therapy consisting of rivaroxaban, prasugrel, and aspirin
span: triple therapy of Rivaroxaban, Prasugrel, and Aspirin

content: Simple nasal packing was used for mild bleeding episodes
Rivaroxaban was renally dosed because of the patient's reduced creatinine clearance of 30

Patient's creatinine clearance of 30 mL/min placed him at the lower boundary of the ROCKET AF inclusion criteria, where patients with CrCl below 30 were excluded and those with 30-49 received a reduced rivaroxaban dose
span: patients with creatinine clearance <30 mL/min were excluded, and those with a creatinine clearance of 30-49 mL/min received a reduced dose of rivaroxaban (15 mg daily)

content: This is believed to be the first case report of bleeding complications involving this combination of medications
span: We believe this is the first case report of bleeding complications involving this combination of medications
